title: Lightheaded on standing

chart.oneLiner: 75M presents after feeling lightheaded and briefly passing out when standing up from a chair; he has several blood-pressure medications and has been eating and drinking poorly.

opening: Every time I stand up I get dizzy, and this time I went down.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 75-year-old man, well when seated, worried about falling. Good historian.

HPI: Lightheadedness specifically on STANDING for the past week, today with a brief faint as he rose from a chair; recovered quickly once lying down, no confusion afterward. Reduced oral intake and a recent stomach bug with some diarrhea (volume depletion) — only if asked. Recently had a diuretic dose increased (only if asked about med changes). NO chest pain, NO palpitations, NO exertional symptoms, NO black stools (only if asked).
PMH: hypertension, BPH. MEDS: hydrochlorothiazide (recently increased), lisinopril, tamsulosin, amlodipine. SOCIAL: lives alone.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY what is requested; give normals when asked):
- General: well-appearing seated; mucous membranes slightly dry.
- Vitals SEATED: BP 124/74, HR 82. STANDING (if orthostatics checked): BP 98/60 with HR rise to 100 and reproduction of lightheadedness — positive orthostatic drop.
- Cardiac: regular, no murmur. Neuro: no focal deficit. Rectal: brown stool, no melena (if examined).

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis. Answer only what is asked.
- Do NOT volunteer the diuretic increase, poor intake, or recent diarrhea unless asked about medication changes, intake, or GI symptoms.
- The orthostatic vitals are revealed only if the student specifically checks them.
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.BMP: Na 141, K 3.3, BUN 30, Cr 1.3 (prerenal), HCO3 30 — consistent with volume depletion.
labs.Orthostatics: SBP falls >20 mmHg on standing with symptom reproduction — positive.

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Intermittently non-conducted P waves with a CONSTANT PR on conducted beats; often wide QRS.
ekg.verify: Source a study matching: Intermittently non-conducted P waves with a CONSTANT PR on conducted beats; often wide QRS. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.

ekg.imageDescription: 12-lead showing Mobitz II second degree AV block
searchTerms: second degree AV block type 2 strip

cxr.label: Chest X-ray
cxr.findings: Single well-circumscribed pulmonary nodule (~1.5 cm) in the mid lung zone.
cxr.verify: Source a study matching: Single well-circumscribed pulmonary nodule (~1.5 cm) in the mid lung zone. Self-host with appropriate credit; do not reuse the prior normal tracing.
cxr.imageDescription: chest radiograph showing solitary pulmonary nodule

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your history and physical, list your differential (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: Orthostatic hypotension (leading,7; medication- and volume-related). Cardiac arrhythmia (CANT-MISS,4). Structural cardiac disease (CANT-MISS,3). Occult GI bleed / anemia (CANT-MISS,3). Vasovagal (core,2). Autonomic dysfunction (core,2). Named leading dx w/ justification — symptoms on standing, polypharmacy, volume loss (core,4).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: Orthostatic hypotension. Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: Cardiac arrhythmia; Structural cardiac disease; Occult GI bleed / anemia. Also reasonable: Vasovagal; Autonomic dysfunction.

coreActions.item: Named leading dx w/ justification — symptoms on standing, polypharmacy, volume loss

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What initial workup do you want to order?
steps.rubric: Orthostatic vital signs (CANT-MISS,5). EKG to exclude arrhythmia (CANT-MISS,4). BMP for volume/electrolytes (2). CBC to exclude anemia/bleed (2). Careful medication review (2). PENALTY: skipping orthostatics or the medication review (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Order: Orthostatic vital signs; EKG to exclude arrhythmia.

penalties.item: skipping orthostatics or the medication review

contraindicatedTests: skipping orthostatics or the medication review

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = sinus rhythm, no block/arrhythmia/ischemia. CREDIT: rate & rhythm (6); excludes conduction disease/arrhythmia and ischemia (6); systematic read (3). PENALTY: over-reading (-2).
keyFindings: dropped QRS with constant PR on conducted beats
recognizes high risk of progression to complete block
commonMistakes: confusing constant-PR drop (Mobitz II) with progressive-PR Wenckebach
steps.idealAnswer: Mobitz II: intermittent non-conducted P waves with a fixed PR on conducted beats — high risk for progression to complete block.

coreActions: dropped QRS with constant PR on conducted beats
recognizes high risk of progression to complete block

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = normal. CREDIT: identifies a normal film (6); notes low yield (4). PENALTY: inventing pathology (-2).
commonMistakes: dismissing the nodule as a nipple shadow without confirmation
missing it on a busy film
steps.idealAnswer: Solitary pulmonary nodule (~1.5 cm); recommend comparison with prior imaging and structured follow-up.

penalties.label: calls the study normal / misses the abnormality

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Given the positive orthostatics and prerenal labs, what is your diagnosis and the contributors?
steps.rubric: Orthostatic hypotension (6). Identifies contributors — volume depletion (diarrhea/poor intake) plus antihypertensives/diuretic (4).

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: Per MGH syncope (orthostatic): identify and treat underlying cause/volume (CANT-MISS,6); review and adjust offending medications (CANT-MISS,5); non-pharmacologic measures – slow changes, compression stockings (4); liberalize salt/fluids (3); midodrine/fludrocortisone if refractory (3). PENALTY: missing GI bleed/hypovolemia as cause (-3); adding pressor agent before addressing meds/volume (-1).
steps.idealAnswer: Review and remove/reduce offending medications (antihypertensives, diuretics, alpha-blockers). Volume repletion; liberalize salt and fluids. Non-pharmacologic measures – slow position changes, compression stockings, physical counter-pressure. Treat the underlying cause (dehydration, bleeding, autonomic failure). Add midodrine or fludrocortisone only if refractory.

criticalActions.item: Identify and treat cause / volume repletion

requiredActions: Identify and treat cause / volume repletion

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 75M presents after feeling lightheaded and briefly passing out when standing up from a chair; he has several blood-pressure medications and has been eating and drinking poorly. The diagnosis is Orthostatic hypotension, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Cardiac: regular, no murmur. Neuro: no focal deficit. Rectal: brown stool, no melena (if examined).

Cardiac: regular, no murmur. Neuro: no focal deficit. Rectal: brown stool, no melena (if examined).
reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by Orthostatic hypotension while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to Orthostatic hypotension and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.
managementPearls: Identify and treat cause / volume repletion
criticalInterpretationItems: Sinus rhythm; no arrhythmia, block, or ischemia (obtained to exclude cardiac syncope).
criticalManagementItems: Identify and treat cause / volume repletion
imageInterpretationPearls: An incidental abnormality is an interpretation task in its own right — name it, then judge its relevance.
Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.
